title: Payment rails
subtitle: Global and regional networks, processors, and settlement systems that move money from cardholders and merchants through acquirers, issuers, and central bank reserves.
financials_note: Five year income statement trends for the chokepoints and major processors. Revenue bars read on the left; margins read on the right. Visa and Mastercard operate as near duopolies with structural pricing power; regional players and stablecoins remain competitive and substitutable despite scale.
chokepoints_note: Six firms the system genuinely cannot route around without geopolitical or operational consequence: the two global card networks, three government or government-anchored payment authorities, and a member cooperative that has been weaponized as a financial blockade. Every other player faces competitive alternatives.

layers.label: Card networks
layers.desc: Global and regional payment networks that set rules and route card transactions

layers.label: Domestic switches
layers.desc: Country level payment switches and networks mandated for domestic transactions

layers.label: Card issuers
layers.desc: Banks and financial institutions that issue cards to consumers

layers.label: Core banking systems
layers.desc: Infrastructure software that runs issuing and settlement

layers.label: Issuer processors
layers.desc: Card processing platforms for fintech and bank card programs

layers.label: Merchant acquirers
layers.desc: Banks and processors that acquire payments from merchants

layers.label: Payment processors
layers.desc: Software vendors that process transactions for banks and networks

layers.label: Payment gateways
layers.desc: Online checkout and payment collection platforms

layers.label: Interbank messaging
layers.desc: Networks for cross-border payment instructions between banks

layers.label: Settlement systems
layers.desc: Central and private clearing houses for transaction settlement

layers.label: Cross-border payment
layers.desc: Platforms for international money transfers and FX

layers.label: Alternative rails
layers.desc: Digital wallets, P2P networks, and non-card payment systems

layers.label: Stablecoin issuers
layers.desc: Crypto platforms issuing dollar-backed digital currencies

One of two networks that clear the large majority of the world's card transactions. Any bank, merchant, or country that wants global card acceptance has no viable substitute.
chokepoint_why.Mastercard Incorporated: The second half of the global card network duopoly with Visa. Together the two route the overwhelming majority of cross-border card volume with no structural substitute.
chokepoint_why.UnionPay: China's mandatory domestic card switch for RMB-denominated transactions, and by transaction value the world's largest card network. No domestic competing network is permitted to operate at scale in China.
chokepoint_why.SWIFT (Society for Worldwide Interbank F: The de facto sole global standard for interbank payment messaging. Disconnecting a country's banks from SWIFT (used against Russian and Iranian banks) functions as a de facto financial blockade, evidence no substitute exists at scale.
chokepoint_why.Federal Reserve System (Fedwire Funds Se: The literal end of the line for US dollar settlement. Every private USD rail ultimately settles through central-bank reserve accounts, which only the Fed can create, with no private alternative by design.
chokepoint_why.NPCI (National Payments Corporation of I: Sole operator of India's UPI real-time payment switch, the domestic rail that essentially all Indian retail digital payments route through, a government-backed monopoly with no permitted alternative.
